tweets.text: The synthetic drug crisis is urgent and global. We are working with partners to save lives at home and abroad. Last month, @SecBlinken launched the Global Coalition to Address Synthetic Drug Threats, and today on #IOAD2023, he outlines our coordinated strategies and solutions.

Illicitly-manufactured synthetic drugs threaten the health, security, and wellbeing of people around the world. Learn more about how the U.S. and the Global Coalition to Address Synthetic Drug Threats will work to combat this issue: https://t.co/dOvExmqe6s

Opioids remain the most lethal group of drugs. Illicit fentanyl, a synthetic opioid, can be 50 times stronger than heroin. The U.S. is leading a coordinated global effort with international partners to disrupt the illicit synthetic drug trade. Learn more: https://t.co/DHzqcfTzFI

On this third @UN International Day for People of #AfricanDescent, the U.S. celebrates the contributions of persons of African descent to our communities and society. We are committed to promoting racial equity and justice both at home and around the world. https://t.co/9VOVKb3DPW

The Kremlin appears determined to erase Ukraine’s existence as a state by attempting to rob it of its future. This month @TheGEC released a new report on the Kremlin’s War Against Ukraine’s Children. #TakenFromUkraine https://t.co/GmEwmHcvaE

.@SecBlinken: “Governments that violate human rights are almost always the same ones that flout other key parts of that order – such as invading, coercing, and threatening other countries, or breaking trade rules.” The U.S. will continue to take a stand for human rights for all. https://t.co/SZjZ4YMsLJ

Russia is forcibly taking Ukrainian children to Russia, putting them in re-education camps, and isolating them from their families. The world has a moral duty to hold Russian leaders accountable. #TakenFromUkraine https://t.co/EYOIx1YBnu

Russia has separated thousands of Ukrainian children from their families or caregivers and held them in camps & facilities where they are exposed to a Russia-centric curriculum, according to a new report from @HRL_YaleSPH. Many children are still missing. https://t.co/nS7r9cnQ7G

On this International Day of the Victims of Enforced Disappearances, we call on governments around the world to put an end to this practice, hold those responsible to account, and respect the human rights and fundamental freedoms of all persons. 
https://t.co/YB6skuAabT https://t.co/TuNauKwTLZ

If you’ve seen the movie Oppenheimer, you might be wondering about what the U.S. is doing today.

@UnderSecT Bonnie Jenkins shares how we are using diplomacy to work towards a world without nuclear weapons. https://t.co/EJYdD52GoE
“Each of us has a responsibility to act.” Earlier this month @SecBlinken spoke to the @UN Security Council about strengthening food security so no one goes hungry. https://t.co/SHFdoAqO8P

Illegal, unreported, and unregulated fishing is a threat to the health of the ocean and all who depend on it. Hear from @SciDiplomacyUSA about how the United States promotes sustainable fisheries — and what you can do to help. https://t.co/XEQJSNqz9U

Interested in representing America in a meaningful career where diversity and inclusion are vital? Learn about @doscareers opportunities in the United States and around the world by visiting https://t.co/CqRHMQ9GyE. We hope you join us! https://t.co/cw3tLBAGrg

60 years ago on August 28, 1963, the Reverend Martin Luther King Jr. delivered a speech that helped propel the passage of the Civil Rights Act. His iconic words about his dream of equality are remembered around the world. More on that day and his message: https://t.co/j25A7tPEzo

.@SecBlinken: We are operating in an extraordinarily diverse world. If we were to leave our own diversity on the sidelines, we’d be shortchanging ourselves, shortchanging our foreign policy, shortchanging the country. We’re determined not to do that. https://t.co/uBfUqI5orl

Planning a trip abroad? Make sure you review the Travel Advisories for your intended destination. You can see the world at a glance on our interactive map. Learn more: #TravelSmartFromTheStart https://t.co/ZUIfgAmxZj https://t.co/yP72pxLvRk
Thinking about traveling abroad? Before confirming your travel plans, review the travel advisory for your destination at https://t.co/yR0YJrH1NO. #TravelSmartFromtheStart https://t.co/1vmDoClrhS

Russia’s attacks on Ukrainian grain silos are devastating for farmers in Ukraine — and for starving people around the world.
So many families can be fed by this vital lifeline if Russia stops its cruel attacks and rejoins the Black Sea Grain Initiative. https://t.co/PTUme4k9bF
Russia continues to prevent Ukrainian grain from reaching those around the world who need it most. Putin simply does not care about global food security. https://t.co/WvvpSr2c7N

.@USAmbUN: In a world abundant with food, no one should ever starve to death – ever. This is a humanitarian issue, this is a moral issue, and this is a security issue. And we must address the most insidious driver of famine and food insecurity: conflict. https://t.co/AvkHPbCrID

The world’s population could reach 10 billion by 2050, which means we must work together to address the growing global food crisis. This month at the @UN Security Council, @SecBlinken announced $362 million in new U.S. assistance to twelve nations. https://t.co/QSkbJlvF42

Today is #WomensEqualityDay, a time to honor all those who fought – and those who continue to fight – for equal rights. We continue our work toward a world that is more equitable and just. https://t.co/dRfOhUxFLF

The United States and Global Equality Fund partners remain committed to protecting and advancing the human rights of LGBTQI+ persons globally. Together with the private sector, GEF has dedicated over $100 million to programs in 100+ countries: https://t.co/cJqyR2SiGP

Americans and international visitors from every corner of the world come to the U.S. to enjoy “the best idea we’ve ever had” — our national parks. Today we celebrate #NPSBirthday with the @NatlParkService! Join us on our road trip to a few of Washington, D.C.’s parks. https://t.co/IlgZMyNLxj

Many of Ukraine’s most powerful symbols were born during the nation’s fight for independence throughout its history, and many are known worldwide.

Here’s a look at five symbols that represent Ukraine’s pride, sacrifice and unity: https://t.co/XPkadH2W1q

Today @SecBlinken joined Ukrainian Foreign Minister @DmytroKuleba and other ministers from around the world in reciting Ukraine’s national anthem, line by line. On Ukraine’s Independence Day and every day, we stand in solidarity. Check out the 🧵

The Ukrainian people continue to demonstrate extraordinary resilience and resolve 18 months into Putin’s brutal war. “Governments and people around the world–the United States proudly among them–have rallied to Ukraine’s side.” -@SecBlinken https://t.co/kFFm2zrPnb

On Ukraine’s Independence Day, the United States remains committed to helping Ukraine achieve a brighter future that is determined by their people, not Russia. Slava Ukraini! #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/FCuz87dH5Z
The Kremlin continues to target and attack Ukraine’s ports and food exports. This time, Russian forces destroyed 13,000 tons of grain that was destined for Egypt and Romania. https://t.co/pT7N4ErOX7

“Inclusion is necessary for durable peace.” @StateCSO Assistant Secretary Anne Witkowsky describes the vital nexus between women’s participation in inclusive peace and the United States’ efforts in #AtrocityPrevention, in our latest DipNote. #WPSinAction https://t.co/UwUaCZHjAL
Human rights are critical to American diplomacy, and for a free, open, prosperous, and secure world. https://t.co/IE9VwWsuvm

Russia’s decision to walk away from the Black Sea Grain Initiative has drastic consequences. The deal delivered grain to some of the world’s most food-insecure countries. World leaders are urging Russia to rejoin the agreement: https://t.co/yp036IqMvS

Since the Russian aggression, a year and a half ago, the United States has provided about $15.5 billion to global food security. We are by far the largest donor to the World Food Programme. We provide 50 percent of its budget. Russia provides less than 1 percent of its budget. https://t.co/3x8pOqsGae

Russia’s war of aggression on Ukraine has exacerbated hunger globally, especially because many countries in Africa & the Middle East are dependent on food from Ukraine. Russia’s withdrawal from the #BlackSeaGrainInitiative has a major impact on the availability of food worldwide. https://t.co/tGNPM74zgj

.@POTUS: “Our countries are stronger and the world will be safer as we stand together." The United States, Japan, and the Republic of Korea affirmed key understandings and committed to expanding cooperation in the Indo-Pacific. More on our cooperation: https://t.co/lH6V1rnIl6 https://t.co/zz7YyRx9S9
The United States is working alongside our global partners to end the catastrophic loss of biodiversity by protecting 30 percent of global lands and waters by 2030. #30x30 https://t.co/C9A9qrV1e4

Freedom of religion or belief is a core American value and a fundamental freedom enshrined in international law. Learn how the United States stands with and supports brave advocates for religious freedom. https://t.co/EPbE5soeKV
.@SecBlinken: When each person is respected for their beliefs, they are more empowered to reach their full potential, which in turn lifts entire communities and societies. https://t.co/BQHJxPCWkF

.@POTUS on the U.S.-Japan-Republic of Korea relationship: In the months and years ahead, we’re going to continue to seize those possibilities together — unwavering in our unity and unmatched in our resolve. https://t.co/PMoHz8Tnl9 https://t.co/qSckCqUpRy

Today is the International Day of Remembrance and Tribute to the Victims of Terrorism. We will never forget the victims of terrorism and are committed to preventing terrorist attacks and the pursuit of justice and peace around the world. https://t.co/WTt3wTPkfM
The U.S. is committed to building a more water-secure world and celebrates those working to ensure safe water for all. #WorldWaterWeek https://t.co/E28lDNGiYS

Russia’s attacks on Ukrainian grain silos are devastating for farmers in Ukraine — and for starving people around the world. So many families can be fed by this vital lifeline if Russia stops its cruel attacks and rejoins the Black Sea Grain Initiative. https://t.co/vIx2vc7Kxf

In “The Week at State,” @POTUS hosts the leaders of Japan and the Republic of Korea at Camp David for a trilateral summit, and we recognize #WorldHumanitarianDay, honoring humanitarian workers around the world who put their lives at risk to deliver life-saving assistance. https://t.co/E5hQ1G5hsT

Did you know one-third of the world’s food supply relies on pollinators? In honor of #WorldHoneybeeDay, take our quiz on pollinators. You won’t bee sorry! You will learn all the ways pollinators create a healthy environment and a strong economy. https://t.co/FFJKrPRucG

The United States combats wildlife trafficking by strengthening law enforcement, reducing demand for illegal wildlife and wildlife products, and building international cooperation.  #EndWildlifeTrafficking https://t.co/prx7MIk29t

On #WorldHumanitarianDay, we honor brave and compassionate humanitarian workers and organizations who aid the world’s most vulnerable. In the face of crises created by conflict, climate disasters, and pandemics, they risk their lives to help others. https://t.co/XJEdSN6IKs

We recognize the sacrifice of humanitarian aid workers around the world and call for immediate, safe, and unhindered access for these workers to deliver critical aid and services – from healthcare and education to safe drinking water and sanitation. https://t.co/phtEvVewE5 https://t.co/pTH2FuyjZ4

.@SecBlinken: We’re expanding our presence in critical regions like the Indo-Pacific, and we’re leading unprecedented coalitions to confront aggression and address humanitarian crises around the world. https://t.co/WjmbJIRkNo

In 2015, after trilateral meetings, then-Deputy Secretary Blinken spoke to the press with Japanese Vice Foreign Minister Saiki and ROK Vice Foreign Minister Cho. Check out this video of @SecBlinken from the archives: https://t.co/8jxuFTlpOv https://t.co/HDB8nNgmXZ

In January 2016, then-Deputy Secretary Blinken at a press conference in Tokyo underscored the “paramount importance of upholding rules and norms that safeguard the stability of the region and the security of all nations.” https://t.co/IQweOLj98W

In 2016, then-Deputy Secretary Blinken had trilateral meetings with Japan and the Republic of Korea in January, April, July, and October. Check out these photos from the meetings. https://t.co/GmLrHmtZgY

@POTUS @SecBlinken In 2017, the three countries emphasized the importance of trilateral cooperation in advancing peace and security, including countering the threat from North Korea. https://t.co/XujXvx9mCr

Tomorrow, @POTUS will host PM Kishida Fumio of Japan and President Yoon Suk Yeol of the Republic of Korea for a trilateral leaders summit at Camp David. #DYK @SecBlinken has been helping to build this relationship with our Pacific neighbors for years? Here’s a throwback 🧵

.@StateDeputySpox previews Ukraine’s Independence Day from the Soviet Union, marked on August 24: This year’s celebrations will be bittersweet for Ukraine’s people because for the past 18 months they have continued to face the Russian government’s onslaught of brutal attacks. https://t.co/yaOVs3WPof

For the past three years, the United States and @USAmbUN Thomas-Greenfield have focused on food insecurity as president of the @UN Security Council. More than 700 million people across the world go to bed hungry. We must take on this global crisis. https://t.co/C0j8QYN76D

.@StateDeputySpox on Russia’s continued attacks on Ukrainian grain infrastructure: This further escalates the global food crisis, and it keeps food prices high.

Our Ukrainian partners are inspiring the world while Russia starves it by weaponizing food. https://t.co/T4MCR1OTgw

The Bialystok Ghetto uprising on August 16, 1943 was one of the countless acts of resistance by Jews in ghettos and concentration camps across Europe to reject dehumanization and reaffirm their dignity. The United States will always be a partner in keeping this history alive. https://t.co/7w2h2DbbGG

We honored six distinguished individuals with the inaugural Global Anti-Racism Champions Awards this month. Hear from the awardees as they explain the important work that they do to make a difference in their communities and the world. #GARC2023 https://t.co/QQ6PuAStDd

.@SecBlinken dropped by the Department’s Press Briefing Room today where he discussed the upcoming U.S.-Japan-ROK trilateral summit, Ukraine, Iran, our commitment to the safety and security of Americans overseas, and took questions from reporters. https://t.co/sQmLLN1o3D

.@SecBlinken on the release of U.S. citizens from Iranian prison to house arrest: I have no higher priority than looking out for the security and well-being of Americans around the world. https://t.co/cMeV4sGdkd

.@SecBlinken outlines Friday’s historic trilateral leader’s summit, where @POTUS will host Prime Minister Fumio Kishida of Japan and Republic of Korea President Yoon Suk Yeol at Camp David in Maryland. https://t.co/vdDlhhnMun

For years, the Kremlin exploited revenue from fossil fuels to fund its authoritarian ambitions. Now, Europe is phasing out Russian fossil fuels by accelerating new energy infrastructure projects and diversifying energy sources, building stability for future generations. https://t.co/jaLnuxmxxk
Human rights are critical to American diplomacy, and for a free, open, prosperous, and secure world. https://t.co/DngSkRHORS

.@SecBlinken: When individuals don’t see themselves reflected in governments purported to represent them, that undermines faith in the system and in democracies. Racism, discrimination–these are not only morally wrong: They make our world less safe, less stable, less prosperous. https://t.co/AKvdtKMCvU

In “The Week at State,” the Department remembers the victims of the 1998 U.S. Embassy terrorist bombings in Kenya and Tanzania, @SecBlinken honors the inaugural Global Anti-Racism Champions and meets with Mexico’s new Foreign Secretary. Subscribe at: https://t.co/gNUp8OnOFr https://t.co/53FPmA6917

Everyone deserves safe water sources. Through the Global Water Strategy, the U.S. is working in 22+ countries on a wide variety of water security issues, including conservation & management of freshwater resources, all with the goal of creating a water-secure world for everyone. https://t.co/vI1346HCZ4

Since the Russian aggression, a year and a half ago, the United States has provided about $15.5 billion to global food security. We are by far the largest donor to the @WFP. We provide 50 percent of its budget. Russia provides less than 1 percent of its budget. https://t.co/t3vvfC25iC

On International #YouthDay, we celebrate emerging leaders. Networks such as @YALINetwork, @ylainetwork & @yseali, and connections formed through @ECAatState programs, help empower young people to play an important role in their communities and the world. https://t.co/08SPjMVT12 https://t.co/vp14204Rsq

We continue to call on countries to close commercial domestic ivory markets. These markets fuel demand for illegal wildlife, provide an avenue to launder illegal ivory, and hinder efforts to detect and combat ivory trafficking globally. #WorldElephantDay https://t.co/kymyolPH5w

The United States is rallying the world to address global food insecurity. Last week, @SecBlinken and @USAmbUN brought the @UN Security Council together to focus on this urgent challenge. More than 90 countries signed a communique to end the use of food as a weapon of war. https://t.co/ZHDo3l0YFI

The African Museology International Exchange Program, part of @HeritageAtState, is strengthening collaboration between #culturalheritage professionals, including recently from 8 African nations. More on our commitment to preserving irreplaceable culture: https://t.co/m8PiUVrvHg
The United States and Mexico are stronger, safer, and more prosperous when we work together. We are partners, neighbors, and friends. https://t.co/jNGYehIssM

.@SecBlinken: Expanding inclusive opportunities and jobs in North America, accelerating North America’s clean energy transition, modernizing our border infrastructure aren’t simply issues that matter to our governments; profoundly, they matter to our people. https://t.co/7hpP2jVpTk

The U.S. and Mexico are working together to protect our citizens and make our nations safer and stronger. We are committed to transforming our cooperation to protect our people, prevent transborder crime and pursue criminal networks. https://t.co/s0z5s72abC

Through the AAAS Science & Technology Policy Fellowship program, fellows work across the State Department and serve in a variety of roles, providing high-quality expertise to inform U.S. foreign policy, diplomacy, and national security. Learn more: https://t.co/iuUfSXrdgb https://t.co/qwYpovD5jq

Today @SecBlinken & @US_SRREJ  honored civil society leaders from around the world with the first Secretary's Award for Global Anti-Racism. Congratulations to the awardees for their advocacy and courage in advancing human rights and combating discrimination in their communities. https://t.co/2YNv5KFNOh

Victorina Luca of Moldova is a human rights lawyer and founder of the Roma Awareness Foundation who is being honored as an #AntiracismChampion for advocating for racial equity and her expertise in inclusion of marginalized and underserved people. #GARC2023 https://t.co/USZZzn6Rqn

Sarswati, a social activist and President of the Dalit Society Development Forum, is being honored as an #AntiracismChampion for advocating on behalf of people most in need, successfully attaining justice & providing a voice for marginalized and underserved people. #GARC2023 https://t.co/rgQxdiWJ1F

Saadia Mosbah of Tunis, is a Tunisian activist who has dedicated her life to fighting racial discrimination and prejudice, and defending the rights of Black Tunisians.  She is being honored as an #AntiracismChampion for her exceptional courage and leadership. #GARC2023 https://t.co/GbTNQP79Xa

Oswaldo Bilbao Lobatón, an Afro-Peruvian activist born in Lima, Peru is being honored as an #AntiracismChampion for his courage and strength to fight for the recognition and rights for Afro-Peruvians. #GARC2023 https://t.co/TEr8qICvg9

Rani Yan Yan of Bangladesh is a human rights defender & women rights activist who is being honored as a #AntiracismChampion for advocating for vulnerable populations facing government-sponsored discrimination, land grabbing, violence & the effects of climate change. #GARC2023

Kari Guajajara of Brazil is being honored as an #AntiracismChampion for promoting Indigenous Peoples’ rights, combating gender-based violence, and protecting the environment. #GARC2023 https://t.co/P3mOepmTLH

#AntiracismChampion Rani Yan Yan on continuing to achieve racial equity and justice: We need to ensure that rule of law prevails in our countries. We need open and democratic governments that are accountable to citizens and within ever shrinking civic spaces in many countries. https://t.co/cakToaLdoj

In “The Week at State,” @SecBlinken launches the new Bureau of Global Health Security and Diplomacy, meets with @WashFellowship at their annual summit in D.C. and then heads to NYC to speak on global food security at the @UN. Visit https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz for the latest. https://t.co/XhqUsRRZGU

The new Global Health Security and Diplomacy Bureau bureau will better position us to share information across the building, trade lessons learned with partners, and drive action and forge a safer, more secure future for our fellow citizens and for people around the world. https://t.co/D2ZLQeXqzQ

We're proud to be a leading contributor to improving global health. Our new Global Health Security and Diplomacy Bureau will focus exclusively on global health issues and will strengthen our ability to advance this key priority, in the U.S. and countries around the world. https://t.co/jojFiTHm9j

If you’ve seen the movie Oppenheimer, you might be wondering about what the U.S. is doing today.

@UnderSecT Bonnie Jenkins shares how we are using diplomacy to work towards a world without nuclear weapons. https://t.co/4KPI2HbZpF

.@SecBlinken: Strengthening food security is essential to realizing the vision of the @UN Charter—to save generations from the scourge of war and reaffirm the dignity and worth of every single human being. https://t.co/bv0wFCRvTQ

Our approach toward Sub-Saharan Africa is focused on what we can do with Africa, not for Africa. It reflects the incredible diversity and influence of the continent and the important role that young Africans especially have in shaping our planet for generations to come. https://t.co/rN9AlbEqjX

.@SecBlinken chaired a @UN Security Council meeting yesterday on global food insecurity, as 91 countries joined the U.S. in committing to end the use of food as a weapon of war. https://t.co/cgGkMMtqv5

In New York, @SecBlinken convened agricultural industry leaders on the global food crisis and discussed long-term solutions to feed the millions of people facing hunger around the world. https://t.co/yhhzPXKHcH

.@SecBlinken: The objective that President Putin set in Ukraine was to erase it from the map, to eliminate its independence, to subsume it into Russia. That failed a long time ago. https://t.co/900yB0YBXY

.@SecBlinken: “Governments that violate human rights are almost always the same ones that flout other key parts of that order – such as invading, coercing, and threatening other countries, or breaking trade rules.” The U.S. will continue to take a stand for human rights for all. https://t.co/9qh0gWdVnT
In our world today, no one should ever starve. Food security is a humanitarian issue, a moral issue, and a national security issue. https://t.co/0YukrLXuh8

.@USAmbUN: We know that climate change has impacted food insecurity. It is something that we feel every day as we look at the patterns of heat across the globe, the patterns of flooding, intensified storms. We absolutely have to work together to address these issues today. https://t.co/jRJrGFdAaz
